categories.destructive_filesystem: Recursive deletion, formatting, wiping, or raw filesystem writes.
categories.credential_exposure: Commands or literals that can reveal credentials or tokens.
categories.network_exfiltration: Remote content passed directly to an executable shell.
categories.privilege_escalation: Elevated deletion or dangerously broad permissions.
categories.package_script_execution: Package installation that may execute third-party scripts.
categories.git_history_rewrite: Git operations that rewrite history or remove recovery data.
categories.production_deploy: Commands that can publish or apply hosted changes.
categories.database_destructive: Statements that remove databases, schemas, tables, or unscoped rows.
categories.shell_obfuscation: Encoded or dynamically evaluated shell execution.
categories.read_only_operation: Recognized local inspection or verification operations.
categories.local_state_change: Commands that may alter local repository or container state.

rules.reason: Recursive forced deletion can destroy a filesystem tree.
Privileged file deletion has a high destructive impact.
World-writable permissions on the filesystem root are unsafe.
Formatting, wiping, or raw copying can destroy filesystem data.
Quiet or forced recursive deletion can remove a complete directory tree.
Dynamic PowerShell evaluation obscures the executed command.
Remote content is piped into dynamic PowerShell execution.
Encoded content hides the shell command being executed.
The Git operation can rewrite history or remove recovery data.
The statement can destroy database structures or all rows in a table.
Reading dotenv files can expose credentials and tokens.
The command contains a credential-shaped literal or assignment.
Remote or substituted content is executed directly by a shell.
Package installation may execute third-party code or modify tooling state.
The command removes or stops local container resources.
The command can alter or move local working tree state.
The command can publish or apply changes to a hosted environment.
Reads repository state without changing it.
Runs the local Python test suite.
Compiles Python sources for syntax validation.
Runs the Atlas verification utility.
Runs Engram diagnostics without writing memory.
Reads GitHub Actions run metadata.
Performs a recognized local read or shell navigation operation.
